title: epidendrum plagiophyllum
intro: Epidendrum plagiophyllum, also known as the false orchid, is a type of flowering plant in the orchid family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs, and providing relief from sinus congestion. In herbal preparations, Epidendrum plagiophyllum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
